meta.description: Glitch Reality AI — comedy prompt database (deadpan absurd humor)
meta.character: a generic humanoid figure in everyday scenarios
meta.note: voice_lines are comedy bits: setup → absurd escalation → funny punchline. Narrator is NOT scared — they're mildly annoyed or casually accepting insanity.

style_suffixes: glitch effect, RGB color shift, reality breaking apart
simulation error overlay, digital artifacts, pixel corruption
time loop glitch, stuttering motion, déjà vu distortion
gravity inversion, physics malfunction, floating debris
morphing objects, liquid geometry, reality texture tearing
mirror world glitch, duplicate reflections, clone artifacts
color drain then oversaturate, reality desaturating then exploding
object phasing through surfaces, ghost trail, afterimage glitch
zoom glitch, sudden jump cut, reality zoom-in distortion
sky and ground swapping, horizon flip, upside-down world

everyday_glitches.hook: When your coffee pours upward instead of down
everyday_glitches.visual_prompt: a cup of coffee pouring upward defying gravity in a kitchen, liquid floating up, glitch effect, RGB color shift, surreal simulation error, vertical 9:16
everyday_glitches.voice_line: So I'm making coffee this morning. Normal Tuesday. Pour the coffee. Except it goes up. Just straight up. Into the ceiling. I'm standing there holding the pot like an idiot watching brown liquid defy physics. I try pouring slower. Still up. Faster. Up. I tried pouring sideways and it does a U-turn and goes up. At this point I'm not even surprised. I just put the mug on the ceiling and it fills perfectly. Had my coffee upside down. Tasted the same honestly. Three stars. Would glitch again.

everyday_glitches.hook: The moment the sky loads the wrong texture
everyday_glitches.visual_prompt: outdoor scene where the sky has wrong texture like a video game loading error, pixelated clouds, digital artifacts, simulation glitch, vertical 9:16
everyday_glitches.voice_line: Walked outside and the sky was in 144p. I'm not joking. The clouds were blocky. Pixelated. Like someone loaded a JPEG from 2003 where the sky should be. And it was tiling. The same cloud pattern repeating across the entire sky like a Minecraft texture pack. I showed my friend and he goes, the sky looks fine dude. I took a photo. Photo looks normal. But MY EYES are seeing pixel art up there. Apparently I'm the only one running the budget version of reality. Great. Cool. Love that for me.

everyday_glitches.hook: When your shadow just leaves without you
everyday_glitches.visual_prompt: person walking on a sunny street but shadow moves independently in wrong direction, uncanny glitch, reality error, simulation artifact, vertical 9:16
everyday_glitches.voice_line: Walking down the street. Noon. Sun overhead. My shadow should be under me. But it's not. It's just walking away. Going the opposite direction. I stop. It keeps going. I wave. It doesn't wave back. Apparently my shadow has places to be and I'm not invited. It turned a corner. I followed it. Gone. Just an empty sidewalk. Shadow ghosted me. Literally. It came back twenty minutes later acting totally normal. Didn't apologize. Didn't explain. Just showed up like it didn't just go on a solo adventure without me. We're not on speaking terms.

everyday_glitches.hook: Mirror shows you from 5 seconds in the future
everyday_glitches.visual_prompt: person standing in front of a bathroom mirror, mirror reflection shows them doing something 5 seconds ahead, time glitch, surreal, vertical 9:16
everyday_glitches.voice_line: I'm brushing my teeth and mirror-me is already rinsing. I haven't started rinsing yet. Mirror-me is five seconds ahead. I raise my hand. Mirror-me already raised it. It's not copying me. I'm copying IT. So naturally I use this power to check if I'm about to do anything stupid. Mirror-me reaches for the towel. I reach for the towel. Mirror-me trips on the bath mat. I step over the bath mat. Mirror-me looks annoyed. Honestly this is the most useful glitch ever. Free spoiler alerts for my own life. Ten out of ten.

everyday_glitches.hook: Your reflection blinks when you don't
everyday_glitches.visual_prompt: close-up of a face looking in a mirror, the reflection blinks slightly off-sync, uncanny glitch effect, eerie simulation error, vertical 9:16
everyday_glitches.voice_line: Here's something fun. I blinked. My reflection blinked half a second late. Like laggy WiFi but for my face. I blinked again. Same delay. So I stared at it without blinking for a full minute. Staring contest with my own reflection. And then it blinked. Without me. A slow, casual blink. While my eyes were wide open. So I did what any sane person would do. I blinked back. Then it winked. Then I winked. Now me and my reflection have this thing going. We're friends now. It's weird but I've had worse roommates.

nature_glitches.hook: Tree hit ctrl+Z and un-grew itself
nature_glitches.visual_prompt: a tree rapidly un-growing, leaves flying back onto branches, wood compressing back into seed, reverse time lapse, glitch effect, simulation rewind, vertical 9:16
nature_glitches.voice_line: There's a tree in my neighbor's yard that started un-growing. Monday it's a full oak. Tuesday the leaves suck back in. Wednesday the branches shrink. Thursday it's a sapling. Friday morning it compresses back into an acorn. My neighbor just mows around the empty spot like nothing happened. Dude, you had a fifty-year-old tree here last week. He goes, what tree? SIR. THE OAK. He looks at me like I'm insane. Meanwhile I have a photo of this man grilling under that tree LAST MONTH. The simulation hit undo and everyone agreed to just go with it. Except me apparently.

nature_glitches.hook: The ocean just stopped. Like pause button stopped.
nature_glitches.visual_prompt: vast ocean where all waves suddenly freeze completely still, perfectly flat glass-like water, eerie silence, reality error, simulation pause, vertical 9:16
nature_glitches.voice_line: I'm at the beach and the ocean just stops. Not frozen. Not ice. Just stopped. Every wave hanging in mid-air like a screensaver. Wind still blowing. Seagulls still vibing. But the entire Pacific Ocean is just glass. I could literally see fish stuck mid-swim. One dude tried to surf the frozen wave and just stood on it like a floor. Works great as a floor actually. Then forty-five seconds later everything crashes back at once. Biggest splash you've ever seen. Everyone on the beach is soaking wet pretending it didn't happen. Ma'am you're drenched. I know what I saw.

nature_glitches.hook: Rain going up but only in this one specific spot
nature_glitches.visual_prompt: rain falling upward in a single column in a park, surrounding area normal, localized gravity anomaly, glitch in reality, surreal, vertical 9:16
nature_glitches.voice_line: It's raining everywhere except this one spot in the park. Actually, it IS raining there. Just upward. Drops coming out of puddles going straight up into the clouds. A perfect six-foot column of reverse rain. I walk into it. Bone dry. Step out. Soaking wet. In. Dry. Out. Wet. This is the world's dumbest umbrella. I stood in the dry column for twenty minutes just watching people get rained on. Some lady under an umbrella gives me a dirty look. Ma'am I found the cheat code. Don't hate the player, hate the bugged simulation.

nature_glitches.hook: Birds buffering mid-flight like a bad stream
nature_glitches.visual_prompt: dozens of birds frozen mid-flight in a blue sky, perfectly still, time stopped, glitch effect, simulation pause, eerie photo-real, vertical 9:16
nature_glitches.voice_line: Looked up and there are thirty birds just frozen mid-flight. Wings out. Not falling. Just buffering. Like the server lagged. Everything else is moving normally. Cars, people, wind. But these birds are just hovering like someone put the sky on pause. I threw a breadcrumb at one. It bounced off. Solid. Just a solid bird-shaped object in the sky. Then twenty seconds later they all move at once. Perfect formation. Perfectly choreographed. Like the loading finished and they all got the same update at once. Birds version two point one just dropped.

physics_glitches.hook: Fire is cold and ice is boiling help
physics_glitches.visual_prompt: candle flame that is cold blue and freezing objects near it while an ice cube melts upward into steam, inverted physics, reality error, glitch, vertical 9:16
physics_glitches.voice_line: Lit a candle. Flame looks normal. Hold my hand over it. Freezing cold. Not warm. Not hot. Genuinely cold. Meanwhile the ice in my glass is boiling. Bubbling. Steaming. In the glass. My coffee is freezing in the microwave. My ice cream is melting in the freezer. Hot things are cold, cold things are hot. Physics said you know what, opposite day. I tried cooking dinner. Turned on the stove. Food got colder. Put it in the freezer. Perfectly cooked chicken in ten minutes. I'm not even mad. This is more efficient honestly. The devs might be onto something.

physics_glitches.hook: Car going forward but wheels spinning backwards
physics_glitches.visual_prompt: car driving forward on a road but wheels spinning backwards, uncanny motion glitch, reality error, surreal CGI quality, vertical 9:16
physics_glitches.voice_line: Driving on the highway doing sixty. Look at my wheels. Spinning backwards. Car is going forward. GPS says north. Speedometer says sixty-two. But every wheel on this car is rotating in the wrong direction. I pull over. Stop. Wheels stop. Start driving. Backwards again. At this point I accept it. I drive twelve miles with backwards wheels. Get home. Check the dashcam. Footage shows wheels spinning forward. So either my eyes are broken or my dashcam is in on it. My mechanic says everything looks fine. The car drives fine. It just looks ridiculous. I'm choosing not to think about it.

physics_glitches.hook: These books have been floating for 4 days now
physics_glitches.visual_prompt: stack of old books levitating exactly one inch above a wooden table, no explanation, slight digital artifact glow underneath, reality glitch, vertical 9:16
physics_glitches.voice_line: There are books on my desk that won't touch the table. They're floating. One inch above the surface. I can put my hand under them. Nothing there. No magnets. No strings. Just floating books. I pushed them down. They bounce back to one inch. Put more books on top. Also float. Moved the table. Books stayed floating in the exact same spot in the air. Table gone. Books still there. Hovering. It's been four days. I've stopped questioning it. They're just my floating books now. My friends think it's a cool decoration. I have decided this is a feature not a bug.

human_glitches.hook: Met my clone at the grocery store
human_glitches.visual_prompt: person walking down a street, identical clone of them walking in the opposite direction, both wearing same clothes, reality duplication glitch, vertical 9:16
human_glitches.voice_line: Walking to the store and I see myself coming from the other direction. Same jacket. Same shoes. Same face. My literal clone. I stop. He stops. We stare at each other. He looks just as confused. Then he just keeps walking. I get to the store and the cashier goes, you were just here five minutes ago. Bought the same things. I get home. Groceries are already in the fridge. So apparently my clone did my shopping for me. Didn't even leave a note. Honestly pretty helpful. If he's going to exist, might as well be useful. Clone-me, if you're watching, we need milk on Thursday.

human_glitches.hook: Everyone in the cafe froze except me somehow
human_glitches.visual_prompt: busy cafe where every single person is frozen mid-action, coffee cups in mid-air, one person standing normally looking around confused, simulation pause, vertical 9:16
human_glitches.voice_line: I'm in a coffee shop and everyone freezes. Not slows down. Full pause. Barista mid-pour, coffee hanging in the air. Guy next to me has a cookie at his mouth. Woman by the door stuck mid-step. Nobody breathing. Nobody blinking. But I'm fine. Moving around normally. So I do what anyone would do. I take a sip of frozen-dude's coffee. Pretty good actually. I walk around for thirty seconds just vibing in a room of human statues. Then everything unfreezes. Mid-conversation. Mid-bite. Nobody noticed. Except the guy whose coffee I sipped. He looked confused. Sorry bro.

human_glitches.hook: My hands went through my keyboard like a ghost
human_glitches.visual_prompt: close-up of hands typing on a laptop keyboard, fingers occasionally phasing through the keys, ghost hands, simulation collision error, digital artifact, vertical 9:16
human_glitches.voice_line: Typing an email and my finger goes through the keyboard. THROUGH it. My fingertip poked out the bottom of the laptop. Pulled it back out, tried again. Through. My hands are phasing through solid objects now. Can't pick up my phone. Can't touch my desk. I'm a ghost apparently. Great. The timing is terrible because I have that email to send. For two minutes I can't touch anything. I'm just standing there waving my ghost hands through furniture like a haunted IKEA display. Then it stops. I finish the email. It's still in my drafts. I'm too shook from the ghost hands to proofread. Whatever.

cosmic_glitches.hook: The moon is rendering at 12 pixels tonight
cosmic_glitches.visual_prompt: nighttime sky with the moon appearing in extremely low pixel resolution like a video game asset, everything else normal quality, LOD glitch, simulation error, vertical 9:16
cosmic_glitches.voice_line: Looked at the moon last night. Pixelated. Not blurry. PIXELATED. Like a Minecraft moon pasted into the real sky. I could count the squares. Maybe twelve pixels across. The stars? Fine. HD. Crystal clear. But the moon is running on a 1998 graphics card. I took a photo. Photo shows a normal moon. My eyes apparently got the budget version. Asked my neighbor if the moon looks weird. She says it looks fine. Ma'am that is a SQUARE. I watched a twelve-pixel moon cross an 8K sky for six hours. Living with different render settings than everyone else is lonely.

cosmic_glitches.hook: Sun rose twice and nobody said anything
cosmic_glitches.visual_prompt: time-lapse of sunrise, sun rises normally, then RESETS back below horizon and rises again, simulation rewind glitch, reality error, surreal sky, vertical 9:16
cosmic_glitches.voice_line: The sun rose this morning. Beautiful. Orange. Pink. Got my coffee. Watched the whole thing. Then it went back down. Not a sunset. It reversed. Sank back below the horizon like someone hit rewind. Stars came back for ten seconds. Then the sun rose AGAIN. Same colors. Same clouds. Exact replay. My clock said six forty-seven both times. I checked three clocks. All said six forty-seven. We got two sunrises today and nobody is talking about it. I tried posting about it online and everyone said it was a normal day. IT HAPPENED TWICE. Some of you saw it. I know you did.

cosmic_glitches.hook: Stars rearranged themselves into a grid at 3am
cosmic_glitches.visual_prompt: nighttime sky where stars slowly align into perfect geometric patterns, triangles hexagons forming in the stars, reality code visible, simulation geometry, vertical 9:16
cosmic_glitches.voice_line: Three AM. Look out the window. The stars are moving. All of them. Slowly drifting into perfect patterns. Triangles. Hexagons. A grid. The entire sky becomes geometric. Every star at equal distances. It looks like a circuit board. Or like someone left developer mode on and I can see the grid lines. Two minutes of this. Then the stars scramble back to random positions. But here's the thing. I checked the constellations after. Some stars are slightly off from where they should be. They didn't quite make it back to their spots. Someone rearranged the sky and did a sloppy job putting it back. Amateur hour.

voice_line_templates: So I'm just {doing_action}, minding my business, and suddenly {weird_thing_happens}. I stop and stare. {description_of_glitch}. Nobody else notices. {funny_reaction}. It lasted {duration} and then everything went back to normal. {deadpan_closer}.

Here's something that happened {time_ago}. I was {location} when {glitch_event}. At first I thought {rational_explanation}. But then {escalation}. {funny_detail}. I tried {reaction}. {absurd_result}. I've accepted this as my life now.

Okay so {day_of_week}, I'm minding my business and {first_sign}. Then {second_sign}. By the time {third_sign}, I just gave up understanding reality. {funny_description}. {other_people_oblivious}. {resigned_acceptance}.

Last {timeframe}, {normal_activity} when reality just broke. {glitch_description}. Watched it happen for {duration}. {attempt_to_interact}. {absurd_response}. When it stopped, {evidence_or_lack}. Not my problem anymore honestly.

This is going to sound unhinged. {setup}. {first_weird_thing}. I tell myself {denial}. Then {second_weird_thing}. At this point I'm not even surprised. {investigation}. {discovery}. {funny_punchline}.
random_details: with hexadecimal code floating in background
